articles.title: Ireland host rare home Test as New Zealand seek red-ball rhythm
articles.description: This will be the first ever Test between the two countries, who have both had little red-ball practice lately

articles.title: Marsh out of Pakistan series with ankle injury, Inglis to captain
articles.description: Stand-in skipper Marsh has been withdrawn due to an ankle injury with his availability for the Bangladesh tour to be determined

articles.title: Lanning goes freelance after opting out of Victoria contract
articles.description: The former Australia captain currently has T20 deals in India and England

articles.title: Sri Lanka name Kusal Mendis ODI and T20I captain for WI tour
articles.description: Wanindu Hasaranga is set to return to action in ODIs and T20Is since tearing his hamstring early in the T20 World Cup in February

articles.title: Gibson, Dean knock over NZ for 80 as England cruise to series win
articles.description: England closed out the chase in the decider with seven wickets and 37 balls to spare

articles.title: Australia women's coach Shelley Nitschke signs three-year contract extension
articles.description: Will remain in her role until June 2029 during a time when more change is likely

articles.title: Devine intervention brings New Zealand back from the dead
articles.description: Pitch-perfect 87 from veteran rescues team from turmoil at 11 for 4, to square series

articles.title: Ireland pick four uncapped players for New Zealand Test
articles.description: The 14-member squad includes Jake Egan, Tom Mayes, Liam McCarthy and Reuben Wilson

articles.title: Babar back for ODIs vs Australia; uncapped Rohail, Minhas and Daniyal called up
articles.description: Shadab Khan and Naseem Shah also returned for three-match series, while Mohammad Rizwan was omitted

articles.title: Bangladesh to tour Zimbabwe for all-format series in June-July
articles.description: Upcoming series will mark Zimbabwe's first home assignment this year, and their first international action since the T20 World Cup

articles.title: Afghanistan Cricket Board announce ODI and one-off Test squads for India tour
articles.description: The one-off Test will start on June 6.
